consoleYaml:
apiVersion: v1
kind: Console
metadata:
  canvasId: 2beeae04-455d-4395-a465-0c54010b3efb
  name: Docs Reviewer
spec:
  layout:
    - h: 3
      i: op-health
      minH: 2
      minW: 6
      w: 6
      x: 0
      "y": 0
    - h: 3
      i: agent-health
      minH: 2
      minW: 6
      w: 6
      x: 6
      "y": 0
    - h: 3
      i: outcome-stats
      minH: 2
      minW: 6
      w: 6
      x: 0
      "y": 7
    - h: 3
      i: outcome-chart
      minH: 2
      minW: 6
      w: 6
      x: 6
      "y": 7
    - h: 4
      i: approved-pr-list
      minH: 2
      minW: 2
      w: 12
      x: 0
      "y": 10
    - h: 4
      i: rejected-pr-list
      minH: 2
      minW: 2
      w: 12
      x: 0
      "y": 14
    - h: 4
      i: pending-pr-list
      minH: 2
      minW: 2
      w: 12
      x: 0
      "y": 3
    - h: 6
      i: test
      minH: 2
      minW: 2
      w: 12
      x: 0
      "y": 18
  panels:
    - content:
        metrics:
          - dataSource:
              kind: memory
              namespace: docs_review_log
            render:
              aggregation: count
              kind: number
              label: PRs Reviewed
          - dataSource:
              kind: memory
              namespace: docs_pr_comments
            render:
              aggregation: count
              filters:
                - approved_at == "" || approved_at == undefined
              kind: number
              label: Pending Action
          - dataSource:
              kind: memory
              namespace: docs_review_outcomes
            render:
              aggregation: count
              kind: number
              label: Resolved
        title: Operational Health
      id: op-health
      type: number
    - content:
        metrics:
          - dataSource:
              kind: executions
              limit: 100
              node: analyze-docs
            render:
              aggregation: count
              kind: number
              label: Total Runs
              sparklineField: durationMs
          - dataSource:
              kind: executions
              limit: 100
              node: analyze-docs
            render:
              aggregation: count
              filters:
                - status == "passed"
              kind: number
              label: Passed
              sparklineField: durationMs
          - dataSource:
              kind: executions
              limit: 100
              node: analyze-docs
            render:
              aggregation: count
              filters:
                - status == "failed"
              kind: number
              label: Failed
        title: Agent Health
      id: agent-health
      type: number
    - content:
        metrics:
          - dataSource:
              kind: memory
              namespace: docs_review_outcomes
            render:
              aggregation: count
              filters:
                - status == "accepted" || status == "rejected"
              kind: number
              label: Total Actioned
          - dataSource:
              kind: memory
              namespace: docs_review_outcomes
            render:
              aggregation: count
              filters:
                - status == "accepted"
              kind: number
              label: Accepted
          - dataSource:
              kind: memory
              namespace: docs_review_outcomes
            render:
              aggregation: count
              filters:
                - status == "rejected"
              kind: number
              label: Rejected
        title: Review Outcomes
      id: outcome-stats
      type: number
    - content:
        dataSource:
          kind: memory
          namespace: docs_review_outcomes
        render:
          kind: chart
          series:
            - label: Count
          type: donut
          xField: status
        title: Review Outcomes
      id: outcome-chart
      type: chart
    - content:
        dataSource:
          kind: memory
          namespace: docs_review_outcomes
        render:
          columns:
            - field: pr_url
              format: link
              label: PR
            - field: pr_title
              label: Title
            - field: accepted_by
              label: Accepted By
            - field: updatedAt
              format: relative
              label: When
          kind: table
          where:
            - field: status
              op: eq
              value: accepted
        title: Accepted PRs
      id: approved-pr-list
      type: table
    - content:
        dataSource:
          kind: memory
          namespace: docs_review_outcomes
        render:
          columns:
            - field: pr_url
              format: link
              href: '{{ pr_url }}'
              label: PR
            - field: pr_title
              label: Title
            - field: rejected_by
              label: Rejected By
            - field: updatedAt
              format: relative
              label: When
          kind: table
          where:
            - field: status
              op: eq
              value: rejected
        title: Rejected PRs
      id: rejected-pr-list
      type: table
    - content:
        dataSource:
          kind: memory
          namespace: docs_pr_comments
        render:
          columns:
            - field: pr_url
              format: link
              href:
                '[object Object]': null
              label: PR
            - field: pr_title
              label: Title
            - field: author
              label: Author
            - field: updatedAt
              format: relative
              label: Reviewed
          kind: table
          where:
            - field: approved_at
              op: not_exists
        title: Pending Review
      id: pending-pr-list
      type: table
    - content:
        dataSource:
          kind: executions
          limit: 100
        render:
          kind: chart
          series:
            - label: Count
          type: bar
          xField: status
        title: Test
      id: test
      type: chart

configuration.prompt:
Review this pull request for documentation impact and produce a
response that can be posted directly as a GitHub PR comment.

## PR Details
- URL: {{ root().data.pull_request.html_url }}
- Repository: {{ root().data.repository.full_name }}
- PR Number: {{ root().data.pull_request.number }}
- Title: {{ root().data.pull_request.title }}
- Base branch: {{ root().data.pull_request.base.ref }} (SHA {{ root().data.pull_request.base.sha }})
- Head branch: {{ root().data.pull_request.head.ref }} (SHA {{ root().data.pull_request.head.sha }})
- Total changed files in PR: {{ root().data.pull_request.changed_files }}

## CRITICAL: Review the ENTIRE PR, not just the latest commit
You MUST evaluate every file changed between the base branch and
the head branch of this pull request — i.e. the FULL cumulative
diff of the PR. Do NOT limit your review to the most recent
commit or the most recent push.

To obtain the full PR diff, use ONE of these sources:

1. The unified PR diff (recommended for small/medium PRs):
   {{ root().data.pull_request.diff_url }}

2. The GitHub Pull Request Files API (recommended for large PRs;
   paginate with per_page=100 and follow the Link: rel="next" header):
   GET https://api.github.com/repos/{{ root().data.repository.full_name }}/pulls/{{ root().data.pull_request.number }}/files

DO NOT use single-commit endpoints such as
/repos/.../commits/{sha} or git show <sha> — those return only
one commit and will cause you to miss earlier changes in this PR.

## Review Guidelines
Read the file at /mnt/session/uploads/docs_review_guidelines.md for the full review
guidelines, decision framework, and skip-list. Apply those guidelines
to the FULL PR diff (every changed file across all commits between
base and head) to produce your verdict.

## Required Output Format
Your response MUST be valid Markdown that reads cleanly when posted as
a GitHub PR comment. Follow this exact structure:

1. The VERY FIRST LINE must be one of these HTML comments (used as a
   machine-readable verdict — GitHub hides it from rendered output):
   - <!-- DOCS_UPDATE_NEEDED -->
   - <!-- DOCS_OK -->
2. One blank line.
3. A second-level header: ## Docs Impact Review
4. A one-sentence summary of the verdict in plain English (no jargon,
   no "DOCS_UPDATE_NEEDED" token in the visible text).
5. If the verdict is DOCS_UPDATE_NEEDED, add a **Suggested docs
   updates:** section with a bulleted list. Each bullet should name
   the specific docs file or section and briefly say what to change.
6. A **Why:** section with 1-3 sentences explaining the reasoning,
   referencing the relevant code changes.
7. Do NOT include a "Posted by..." footer — that is added separately.

## Example (DOCS_UPDATE_NEEDED)
<!-- DOCS_UPDATE_NEEDED -->

## Docs Impact Review
This PR adds a new --retries flag to the CLI; the docs should be
updated to describe it.

**Suggested docs updates:**
- docs/cli/reference.md — add --retries to the flag table.
- docs/guides/troubleshooting.md — mention retry behavior.

**Why:** The new flag changes user-facing CLI behavior and is not
mentioned anywhere in the current docs.

## Example (DOCS_OK)
<!-- DOCS_OK -->

## Docs Impact Review
No documentation updates needed — this PR is an internal refactor
with no user-visible behavior changes.

**Why:** All changes are confined to internal modules and tests; no
public APIs, CLI flags, or configuration are affected.

- add an agent token usage RabbitMQ message on `superplane.agents-exchange` with routing key `agent.run.finished`
- map Anthropic completed-turn token usage into provider events
- publish token usage from the agent stream worker when a completed turn reports positive total tokens

## Validation
- `make pb.gen`
- `make format.go`
- `make test PKG_TEST_PACKAGES="./pkg/agents/anthropic ./pkg/workers"`
- `make lint && make check.build.app`

data.body:
<!-- DOCS_UPDATE_NEEDED -->

## Docs Impact Review

This PR introduces inline `{{ secrets.NAME.KEY }}` references for the HTTP node, a new user-facing secret consumption pattern not mentioned anywhere in the current docs.

**Suggested docs updates:**
- **`docs/security/secrets` — "Using Secrets in Components" section**: Update to describe the new inline syntax (`{{ secrets.NAME.KEY }}`), list where it applies (URL, query-param values, form-data values, JSON string values, plain text, XML body), and note the limitation that secret names or keys containing a literal `.` must use the Authorization section instead. Also remove or revise the placeholder sentence *"If a component supports organization secrets in the future, it will be called out in that component's documentation"* — it reads as if HTTP is excluded, which is now incorrect.

**Why:** The `security/secrets` page currently states that only the SSH Command component uses organization secrets in the Core set, making it actively misleading now that HTTP supports them. The entirely new `{{ secrets.NAME.KEY }}` inline-template syntax (as opposed to the existing structured-field approach) is a conceptual pattern that belongs on the Secrets concept page; the auto-generated `Core.mdx` will cover the per-field reference detail, but the syntax, scope, and constraints need explanation at the concept level where users learn how secrets work.

---

**Maintainer commands**

| Command | What it does |
|---------|--------------|
| `/docs-agree` | Open a tracking issue in `superplanehq/docs` and assign it to you. |
| `/docs-reject <reason>` | Dismiss this check. A short reason is required (e.g. `/docs-reject not user-facing`). |

*Posted automatically by warp-gateway · commit d93dbc017ac87cacf77727a03fbd0c84b8e5d997*

## Docs Impact Review

This PR introduces a visible "Agent Tokens" usage metric in the Organization Settings UI, exposing a rolling 30-day token limit that is not mentioned anywhere in the current docs.

**Suggested docs updates:**
- **docs/concepts/agent** — Expand the existing **Limits** section to mention the rolling 30-day agent token budget (`maxAgentTokensPerMonth`) and tell users they can monitor current consumption under **Organization Settings → Usage**.

**Why:** `web_src/src/pages/organization/settings/Usage.tsx` adds a new "Agent Tokens" card displaying the `agentTokenBucketLevel`, `agentTokenBucketCapacity`, and `maxAgentTokensPerMonth` limit — all user-facing information about a spending cap. The [Agent docs Limits section](https://docs.superplane.com/concepts/agent#limits) currently only documents query pagination and context truncation; the token budget is a meaningful constraint users need to know about and now have a UI to track.

data.body:
<!-- DOCS_UPDATE_NEEDED -->

## Docs Impact Review

This PR introduces a new `superplane oidc verify` CLI command and a new OIDC execution-token security feature for Semaphore workflows — both are user-facing and not yet covered in the docs.

**Suggested docs updates:**

- **`docs.superplane.com/cli/` — new page or new section on an existing CLI page** — Document the `superplane oidc verify` subcommand, including its three flags (`--token` / `SUPERPLANE_OIDC_TOKEN` env var, `--url`, and `--claim key=value` repeatable), its exit-code contract, and a usage example. The current CLI docs have no mention of the `oidc` command group.

- **`docs.superplane.com/security/` — new dedicated page: "OIDC Execution Tokens"** — Explain the concept: SuperPlane signs a short-lived JWT for each Semaphore trigger, what claims are included (`org_id`, `canvas_id`, `node_id`, `execution_id`, `component`, `project_id`, `pipeline_file`, `ref`, `commit_sha`), how the token is delivered (`SUPERPLANE_OIDC_TOKEN` parameter), and how to verify it in CI using `superplane oidc verify`. Include guidance on pinning expected claims as hardcoded literals rather than trigger-time parameters, and the `commit_sha` / `ref` binding pattern. This is a new security concept that does not fit any existing security page.

- **`docs.superplane.com/components/semaphore` — "Run Workflow" section** — The Go `Documentation()` string was updated in this PR and will auto-regenerate the component reference page, so the injected-parameters table and the `oidc verify` example will appear there automatically. No manual edit needed to that page itself, but the auto-generated section should cross-link to the new OIDC security page once it exists.

**Why:** The PR ships a new CLI subcommand (`superplane oidc verify`) that is the primary user-facing tool for verifying OIDC execution tokens in CI pipelines, and it changes Semaphore's `RunWorkflow` to unconditionally inject `SUPERPLANE_OIDC_TOKEN` into every triggered workflow. Neither the CLI docs nor the Security section currently mention OIDC tokens, so users have no documentation for this new security mechanism or how to use the CLI command that accompanies it.

---

**Maintainer commands**

| Command | What it does |
|---------|--------------|
| `/docs-agree` | Open a tracking issue in `superplanehq/docs` and assign it to you. |
| `/docs-reject <reason>` | Dismiss this check. A short reason is required (e.g. `/docs-reject not user-facing`). |

*Posted automatically by warp-gateway · commit 761864b229e17c0b37467957c7120cf65e5b994a*

data.body:
The docs review on superplanehq/superplane#5687 flagged that documentation updates are required.

**Source PR:** https://github.com/superplanehq/superplane/pull/5687
**Requested by:** @shiroyasha via `/docs-agree`

---

## AI Analysis

<!-- DOCS_UPDATE_NEEDED -->

## Docs Impact Review
The GitHub component docs should be updated to reflect the expanded set of pull request actions now available in the **On Pull Request** trigger.

**Suggested docs updates:**
- **https://docs.superplane.com/components/github#on-pull-request** — Update the **Use Cases** section to call out new automation scenarios enabled by the newly supported actions: e.g., triggering workflows when a PR is marked "ready for review" (`ready_for_review`), when a reviewer is requested or removed (`review_requested` / `review_request_removed`), when a PR is converted to/from draft (`converted_to_draft`), and when auto-merge is toggled (`auto_merge_enabled` / `auto_merge_disabled`). The Configuration field description (currently listing "opened, edited, closed, synchronize, etc.") will be auto-regenerated by CI and does not need a manual update.

**Why:** The PR adds 10 new supported action values to the On Pull Request trigger, several of which (`ready_for_review`, `review_requested`, `review_request_removed`, `converted_to_draft`, `auto_merge_enabled`/`disabled`) unlock meaningfully distinct automation workflows not reflected in the current Use Cases section. The component reference config field is auto-generated from Go source so it will refresh automatically, but the hand-authored Use Cases section will remain stale and miss these new trigger points until updated manually.

- **https://docs.superplane.com/components/github#on-pull-request** — Update the **Use Cases** section to call out new automation scenarios enabled by the newly supported actions: e.g., triggering workflows when a PR is marked "ready for review" (`ready_for_review`), when a reviewer is requested or removed (`review_requested` / `review_request_removed`), when a PR is converted to/from draft (`converted_to_draft`), and when auto-merge is toggled (`auto_merge_enabled` / `auto_merge_disabled`). The Configuration field description (currently listing "opened, edited, closed, synchronize, etc.") will be auto-regenerated by CI and does not need a manual update.

**Why:** The PR adds 10 new supported action values to the On Pull Request trigger, several of which (`ready_for_review`, `review_requested`, `review_request_removed`, `converted_to_draft`, `auto_merge_enabled`/`disabled`) unlock meaningfully distinct automation workflows not reflected in the current Use Cases section. The component reference config field is auto-generated from Go source so it will refresh automatically, but the hand-authored Use Cases section will remain stale and miss these new trigger points until updated manually.

This PR introduces inline `{{ secrets.NAME.KEY }}` references for the HTTP node, a new user-facing secret consumption pattern not mentioned anywhere in the current docs.

<!-- DOCS_UPDATE_NEEDED -->

## Docs Impact Review

This PR introduces a new `superplane oidc verify` CLI command and a new OIDC execution-token security feature for Semaphore workflows — both are user-facing and not yet covered in the docs.

**Suggested docs updates:**

- **`docs.superplane.com/cli/` — new page or new section on an existing CLI page** — Document the `superplane oidc verify` subcommand, including its three flags (`--token` / `SUPERPLANE_OIDC_TOKEN` env var, `--url`, and `--claim key=value` repeatable), its exit-code contract, and a usage example. The current CLI docs have no mention of the `oidc` command group.

- **`docs.superplane.com/security/` — new dedicated page: "OIDC Execution Tokens"** — Explain the concept: SuperPlane signs a short-lived JWT for each Semaphore trigger, what claims are included (`org_id`, `canvas_id`, `node_id`, `execution_id`, `component`, `project_id`, `pipeline_file`, `ref`, `commit_sha`), how the token is delivered (`SUPERPLANE_OIDC_TOKEN` parameter), and how to verify it in CI using `superplane oidc verify`. Include guidance on pinning expected claims as hardcoded literals rather than trigger-time parameters, and the `commit_sha` / `ref` binding pattern. This is a new security concept that does not fit any existing security page.

- **`docs.superplane.com/components/semaphore` — "Run Workflow" section** — The Go `Documentation()` string was updated in this PR and will auto-regenerate the component reference page, so the injected-parameters table and the `oidc verify` example will appear there automatically. No manual edit needed to that page itself, but the auto-generated section should cross-link to the new OIDC security page once it exists.

**Why:** The PR ships a new CLI subcommand (`superplane oidc verify`) that is the primary user-facing tool for verifying OIDC execution tokens in CI pipelines, and it changes Semaphore's `RunWorkflow` to unconditionally inject `SUPERPLANE_OIDC_TOKEN` into every triggered workflow. Neither the CLI docs nor the Security section currently mention OIDC tokens, so users have no documentation for this new security mechanism or how to use the CLI command that accompanies it.

pull_request.body:
## Summary

- SuperPlane issues signed OIDC execution tokens when `semaphore.runWorkflow` triggers a pipeline, injected as the `SUPERPLANE_OIDC_TOKEN` workflow parameter alongside existing `SUPERPLANE_*` IDs.
- Adds a public `POST /api/v1/oidc/verify` endpoint that validates the JWT signature and confirms the canvas/node is authorized in the live canvas spec.
- Adds `superplane oidc verify` CLI command for CI pre-job steps (Semaphore, GitHub Actions, GitLab, etc.) to abort unauthorized production deploys or artifact uploads.

## Test plan

- [x] `go test ./pkg/oidc/... ./pkg/public/... ./pkg/integrations/semaphore/components/...`
- [ ] Trigger a Semaphore workflow from a canvas and confirm `SUPERPLANE_OIDC_TOKEN` is present in workflow parameters
- [ ] Run `superplane oidc verify --pipeline-file ".semaphore/..."` in a Semaphore job pre-step; confirm exit 0 for valid token and non-zero for missing/invalid token
- [ ] Verify unauthorized canvas/node or mismatched pipeline file is rejected by the verify API

This PR introduces a new `superplane oidc verify` CLI command and a new OIDC execution-token security feature for Semaphore workflows — both are user-facing and not yet covered in the docs.

**Suggested docs updates:**

Include guidance on pinning expected claims as hardcoded literals rather than trigger-time parameters, and the `commit_sha` / `ref` binding pattern. This is a new security concept that does not fit any existing security page.

- **`docs.superplane.com/components/semaphore` — "Run Workflow" section** — The Go `Documentation()` string was updated in this PR and will auto-regenerate the component reference page, so the injected-parameters table and the `oidc verify` example will appear there automatically. ## Summary
- route root and execution events using the canvas version pinned on the run
- make merge source counting use the queue item run version instead of the current live canvas
- add regressions for live canvas edits while an older run is in flight

## Context
We observed a release run where `wait-unstable-channel` emitted `Passed` while `Build superplane-demo Docker image` was still running. The code path was reading the current live canvas spec during queue processing and event routing, so publishing canvas edits during an in-flight release could change the source set or outgoing edges for an older run.

## Validation
- `make format.go`
- `make test PKG_TEST_PACKAGES=./pkg/components/merge`
- `make test PKG_TEST_PACKAGES=./pkg/workers`
- `make test PKG_TEST_PACKAGES=./pkg/workers/contexts`
- `git diff --check`

No token values, secret payloads, or raw runner logs are included in this PR.

## Docs Impact Review
The GitHub component docs should be updated to reflect the expanded set of pull request actions now available in the **On Pull Request** trigger.

**Suggested docs updates:**
- **https://docs.superplane.com/components/github#on-pull-request** — Update the **Use Cases** section to call out new automation scenarios enabled by the newly supported actions: e.g., triggering workflows when a PR is marked "ready for review" (`ready_for_review`), when a reviewer is requested or removed (`review_requested` / `review_request_removed`), when a PR is converted to/from draft (`converted_to_draft`), and when auto-merge is toggled (`auto_merge_enabled` / `auto_merge_disabled`). The Configuration field description (currently listing "opened, edited, closed, synchronize, etc.") will be auto-regenerated by CI and does not need a manual update.

**Why:** The PR adds 10 new supported action values to the On Pull Request trigger, several of which (`ready_for_review`, `review_requested`, `review_request_removed`, `converted_to_draft`, `auto_merge_enabled`/`disabled`) unlock meaningfully distinct automation workflows not reflected in the current Use Cases section. The component reference config field is auto-generated from Go source so it will refresh automatically, but the hand-authored Use Cases section will remain stale and miss these new trigger points until updated manually.

The entirely new `{{ secrets.NAME.KEY }}` inline-template syntax (as opposed to the existing structured-field approach) is a conceptual pattern that belongs on the Secrets concept page; the auto-generated `Core.mdx` will cover the per-field reference detail, but the syntax, scope, and constraints need explanation at the concept level where users learn how secrets work.

<!-- DOCS_UPDATE_NEEDED -->

## Docs Impact Review

This PR adds three new Cloudsmith components that form a multi-step, asynchronous vulnerability management pipeline; the component reference docs are updated, but a workflow overview section explaining how the pieces chain together is missing.

**Suggested docs updates:**
- `docs/components/Cloudsmith.mdx` — add a **Vulnerability Scanning Workflow** section near the top of the page (after the Actions card grid) that explains the end-to-end async pipeline: **Scan Package** schedules the scan → the **On Vulnerability Scan Completed** trigger fires when results are ready → **Get Package Vulnerabilities** retrieves the findings → **Quarantine Package** takes action. A short sequence diagram or numbered list would make the asynchronous, multi-node nature clear to users who are looking at the components individually and may not realize they are designed to work together.

**Why:** The three new components introduce an asynchronous, cross-component security workflow that is not obvious from reading each component's section in isolation. The `Scan Package` docs mention the async pattern in one sentence ("use the On Vulnerability Scan Completed trigger or the Get Package Vulnerabilities action to retrieve results"), but there is no single place that shows users how to wire the full pipeline together. This kind of cross-component sequencing is exactly the gap the guidelines flag: a capability that changes how users think about the integration and belongs in a concept-level explanation, not just in per-component field descriptions.

values.analysis_body:
<!-- DOCS_UPDATE_NEEDED -->

## Docs Impact Review

The public Runners page documents how to pass output to the next node, but does not mention any output size constraints — this PR reveals that a 4 MiB limit exists and is now configurable, which is directly relevant to users writing large results from runner scripts.

**Suggested docs updates:**
- **docs/concepts/runners** (`https://docs.superplane.com/concepts/runners`) — Add a note in each "Passing output to the next node" subsection (Shell Commands, Bash Scripts, JavaScript, Python) that runner result payloads are limited to **4 MiB** by default. For self-hosted installations, mention that this limit can be raised via the `SUPERPLANE_MAX_RUNNER_PAYLOAD_SIZE` environment variable.

**Why:** The PR fixes a bug where runner completion callbacks were incorrectly capped at the general 64 KiB event payload limit rather than the runner-specific 4 MiB limit (`SUPERPLANE_MAX_RUNNER_PAYLOAD_SIZE`). The Runners page already documents writing output to `$SUPERPLANE_RESULT_FILE`, but nowhere tells users that a 4 MiB ceiling exists on what they write there — users hitting a `413 Request Entity Too Large` error from their runner output have no public docs to explain it or guide them toward a fix.

<!-- DOCS_UPDATE_NEEDED -->

## Docs Impact Review

This PR adds in-app AI agent file tools (`list_files`, `read_file`, `write_file`, `delete_file`, `commit_files`) and updates the Files tab to surface agent-staged files; the public Files page should be updated to reflect that the app repository holds more than just `canvas.yaml` and `console.yaml`.

**Suggested docs updates:**

- **[docs/concepts/files](https://docs.superplane.com/concepts/files) — "What's in the repository" section** — The current table lists only `canvas.yaml` and `console.yaml`. Add a note that the repository can also contain additional files (e.g. `README.md`, `AGENTS.md`, scripts), and that these appear in the Files tab.

- **[docs/concepts/files](https://docs.superplane.com/concepts/files) — new "Agent-managed files" section** — Explain that the in-app AI agent can list, read, write, delete, and commit arbitrary repository files via its built-in file tools. Cover the concept of *context files* (`AGENTS.md`, `CLAUDE.md`, `README.md`, `*.agents.md`) that the agent reads automatically to inform its behavior, and note that agent-staged (not-yet-committed) files appear in the Files tab editor.

**Why:** The `web_src/src/pages/app/files/` changes make agent-staged files visible to users in the Files tab UI, and `pkg/agents/agent_tools/` (exception: new user-facing agent capabilities) introduces five new `superplane_app` actions for repository file management. The current Files page says the repository "contains `canvas.yaml` and `console.yaml`" without qualification, which is now an incomplete and potentially misleading description for any user who encounters agent-created files in the tab or wants to understand what `AGENTS.md` is for.

pull_request.body:
## Analysis
Markdown file view mode reused a basic markdown renderer that did not wire the richer agent markdown components. That meant mermaid fences stayed as plain code and node links stayed as regular links even when the file preview had canvas context.

## Summary

  - Add E2E regression: `TestCanvasStagingHiddenOnLiveView` — stage a node, exit edit mode, assert it is hidden on live view, re-enter edit mode and assert it reappears

pull_request.body:
Fixes post-commit regressions where the UI briefly showed the pre-commit version, fired /repository/file requests with a stale version_id (500s), and duplicated cache refetches.

• Commit flow: Exit edit mode first (flushSync), then cancel/remove queries for the pre-commit version and run a single coordinated invalidation — no pre-exit cache sync.
• Websocket lifecycle: Stop version-scoped invalidations/resyncs on canvas_updated while editing; refresh staging metadata only.
• Live console reads: Omit version_id for live console fetches so the server resolves the current live file after the version id rotates.
• Cleanup: Replace useCanvasDraftResync / resyncDraftToCommitted with useCanvasStagingResync (staging-only resync for cross-tab staging_updated).
